revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「[[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[明石海峡]]に面した[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[明石市]]）]] '''海浜型前方後円墳'''（かいひんがたぜんぽうこうえんふん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の主要な墳形である[[前方後円墳]]の中で、[[リアス式海岸]]の先端や[[海岸段丘]]、[[浜堤|浜堤帯]]など、[[海洋]]の沿岸部に面し、かつ海側からの眺望…」
revision.text:
[[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[明石海峡]]に面した[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[明石市]]）]]
'''海浜型前方後円墳'''（かいひんがたぜんぽうこうえんふん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の主要な墳形である[[前方後円墳]]の中で、[[リアス式海岸]]の先端や[[海岸段丘]]、[[浜堤|浜堤帯]]など、[[海洋]]の沿岸部に面し、かつ海側からの眺望に優れた場所に意図的に築造されたものを表す呼称である。[[考古学者]]の[[広瀬和雄]]が2013年（平成25年）から2015年（平成27年）ごろ提唱した概念とされる{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==概要== [[前方後円墳]]に代表される古墳時代の古墳について、海に面した場所に築造される事例があることは知られていたが、2013年（平成25年）11月に[[神奈川県]]において[[かながわ考古学財団]]主催で開催された[[考古学]]系シンポジウム「海浜型前方後円墳の時代」に際し、広瀬和雄が「海浜型前方後円墳」の呼称を提起し、その定義について具体的に考証を加えた。このシンポジウム記録は2015年（平成27年）に一般書籍化されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015}}。
広瀬は、海浜型前方後円墳の定義として以下の条件を挙げている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=1-31}}。
#「海浜」に面した場所に造られ、かつ周囲より一段「小高い」位置に立地する。海上からも墳丘が見える位置にあり、かつ交通の要衝に立地する。 #海に突き出た高い土地にあり、[[水稲耕作]]に適した[[沖積平野]]の近くには存在しない（そのような沖積平野を望む位置に造られる前方後円墳とは、生産基盤のあり方が異なるらしい）。 #地域の中でも大型の前方後円墳である（※[[円墳]]など、前方後円墳以外の墳形のものも大型の傾向がある）。 #古墳時代前期（4世紀）の段階で、段築構造・[[埴輪]]列・[[葺石]]など、外表施設・構造物を完備した前方後円墳として出現する。 #それまで古墳が存在しなかった地域に突如現れ、首長系譜が1～2代程度しか継続せず（1基単独か、継続しても2基程度しか築造されない）、系譜的連続性に乏しいものが多い。短い首長系譜が一旦途切れ、空白期間を経て突然復活する例もある。 #地域的遍在性をもつ。
[[西日本]]では[[九州]]東西沿岸部、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[大阪湾]]岸、東日本では[[東京湾]]沿岸、かつて存在した[[香取海]]沿岸、[[東北地方]]の太平洋岸に造られた古墳（群）がこれにあたるとされている。海浜型前方後円墳は、海上からの視認性を重視し、[[入り江]]付近や[[岬]]の突端など、海上交通の要衝と言うべき地を選んで造られていると見られることから、これらを築造した人物は、地域において海運を掌握した[[首長]]層と考えられている。またそれまで古墳を造るような首長のいなかった土地にも突如出現し、短期間で首長系譜が途絶える状況から、築造の背後に[[ヤマト王権|中央の王権]]との強い結び付きがあったとも想定されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=21-25}}。
考古学研究者の西川修一は、[[箱式石棺]]墓などの、高塚[[古墳]]以外の墓形・墓制も含めた概念である「海の古墳」よりも範囲を限定した言葉であると位置付けている{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==代表的な例== *[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[明石市]]） *[[網野銚子山古墳]]（[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]） *[[長柄桜山古墳群]]（[[神奈川県]][[逗子市]]・[[三浦郡]][[葉山町]]） *[[内裏塚古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[富津市]]） *[[姉崎古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[市原市]]） *[[磯浜古墳群]]（[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[大洗町]]）
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人[[かながわ考古学財団]]|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher=[[同成社]]|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|ncid=9784886216922|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor=[[神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター]]|title=平成30年（2018年）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=神奈川県教育委員会|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv}}
==関連項目== *[[前方後円墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいひんかたせんほうこうえんふん}}
{{考古学}} [[Category:日本の考古学]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

広瀬は、海浜型前方後円墳の定義として以下の条件を挙げている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=1-31}}。
#「海浜」に面した場所に造られ、かつ周囲より一段「小高い」位置に立地する。海上からも墳丘が見える位置にあり、かつ交通の要衝に立地する。 #海に突き出た高い土地にあり、[[水稲耕作]]に適した[[沖積平野]]の近くには存在しない（そのような沖積平野を望む位置に造られる前方後円墳とは、生産基盤のあり方が異なるらしい）。 #地域の中でも大型の前方後円墳である（※[[円墳]]など、前方後円墳以外の墳形のものも大型の傾向がある）。 #古墳時代前期（4世紀）の段階で、段築構造・[[埴輪]]列・[[葺石]]など、外表施設・構造物を完備した前方後円墳として出現する。 #それまで古墳が存在しなかった地域に突如現れ、首長系譜が1～2代程度しか継続せず（1基単独か、継続しても2基程度しか築造されない）、系譜的連続性に乏しいものが多い。短い首長系譜が一旦途切れ、空白期間を経て突然復活する例もある。 #地域的遍在性をもつ。
[[西日本]]では[[九州]]東西沿岸部、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[大阪湾]]岸、東日本では[[東京湾]]沿岸、かつて存在した[[香取海]]沿岸、[[東北地方]]の太平洋岸に造られた古墳（群）がこれにあたるとされている。海浜型前方後円墳は、海上からの視認性を重視し、[[入り江]]付近や[[岬]]の突端など、海上交通の要衝と言うべき地を選んで造られていると見られることから、これらを築造した人物は、地域において海運を掌握した[[首長]]層と考えられている。またそれまで古墳を造るような首長のいなかった土地にも突如出現し、短期間で首長系譜が途絶える状況から、築造の背後に[[ヤマト王権|中央の王権]]との強い結び付きがあったとも想定されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=21-25}}。
考古学研究者の西川修一は、[[箱式石棺]]墓などの、高塚[[古墳]]以外の墓形・墓制も含めた概念である「海の古墳」よりも範囲を限定した言葉であると位置付けている{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==代表的な例== *[[妙見山古墳 (今治市)|妙見山古墳]]（[[愛媛県]][[今治市]][[大西町]]） *[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]） *[[網野銚子山古墳]]（[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]） *[[長柄桜山古墳群]]（[[神奈川県]][[逗子市]]・[[三浦郡]][[葉山町]]） *[[内裏塚古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[富津市]]） *[[姉崎古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[市原市]]） *[[磯浜古墳群]]（[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[大洗町]]）
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人[[かながわ考古学財団]]|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher=[[同成社]]|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|ncid=9784886216922|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor=[[神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター]]|title=平成30年（2018年）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=神奈川県教育委員会|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv}}

[[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[明石海峡]]に面した[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]）]]
'''海浜型前方後円墳'''（かいひんがたぜんぽうこうえんふん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の主要な墳形である[[前方後円墳]]の中で、[[リアス式海岸]]の先端や[[海岸段丘]]、[[浜堤|浜堤帯]]など、[[海洋]]の沿岸部に面し、かつ海側からの眺望に優れた場所に意図的に築造されたものを表す呼称である。[[考古学者]]の[[広瀬和雄]]が2013年（平成25年）から2015年（平成27年）ごろ提唱した概念とされる{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==概要== [[前方後円墳]]に代表される古墳時代の古墳について、海に面した場所に築造される事例があることは知られていたが、2013年（平成25年）11月に[[神奈川県]]において[[かながわ考古学財団]]主催で開催された[[考古学]]系シンポジウム「海浜型前方後円墳の時代」に際し、広瀬和雄が「海浜型前方後円墳」の呼称を提起し、その定義について具体的に考証を加えた。このシンポジウム記録は2015年（平成27年）に一般書籍化されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015}}。
広瀬は、海浜型前方後円墳の定義として以下の条件を挙げている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=1-31}}。
#「海浜」に面した場所に造られ、かつ周囲より一段「小高い」位置に立地する。海上からも墳丘が見える位置にあり、かつ交通の要衝に立地する。 #海に突き出た高い土地にあり、[[水稲耕作]]に適した[[沖積平野]]の近くには存在しない（そのような沖積平野を望む位置に造られる前方後円墳とは、生産基盤のあり方が異なるらしい）。 #地域の中でも大型の前方後円墳である（※[[円墳]]など、前方後円墳以外の墳形のものも大型の傾向がある）。 #古墳時代前期（4世紀）の段階で、段築構造・[[埴輪]]列・[[葺石]]など、外表施設・構造物を完備した前方後円墳として出現する。 #それまで古墳が存在しなかった地域に突如現れ、首長系譜が1～2代程度しか継続せず（1基単独か、継続しても2基程度しか築造されない）、系譜的連続性に乏しいものが多い。短い首長系譜が一旦途切れ、空白期間を経て突然復活する例もある。 #地域的遍在性をもつ。
[[西日本]]では[[九州]]東西沿岸部、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[大阪湾]]岸、東日本では[[東京湾]]沿岸、かつて存在した[[香取海]]沿岸、[[東北地方]]の太平洋岸に造られた古墳（群）がこれにあたるとされている。海浜型前方後円墳は、海上からの視認性を重視し、[[入り江]]付近や[[岬]]の突端など、海上交通の要衝と言うべき地を選んで造られていると見られることから、これらを築造した人物は、地域において海運を掌握した[[首長]]層と考えられている。またそれまで古墳を造るような首長のいなかった土地にも突如出現し、短期間で首長系譜が途絶える状況から、築造の背後に[[ヤマト王権|中央の王権]]との強い結び付きがあったとも想定されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=21-25}}。
考古学研究者の西川修一は、[[箱式石棺]]墓などの、高塚[[古墳]]以外の墓形・墓制も含めた概念である「海の古墳」よりも範囲を限定した言葉であると位置付けている{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==代表的な例== *[[妙見山古墳 (今治市)|妙見山古墳]]（[[愛媛県]][[今治市]][[大西町]]） *[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]） *[[網野銚子山古墳]]（[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]） *[[長柄桜山古墳群]]（[[神奈川県]][[逗子市]]・[[三浦郡]][[葉山町]]） *[[内裏塚古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[富津市]]） *[[姉崎古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[市原市]]） *[[磯浜古墳群]]（[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[大洗町]]）
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人[[かながわ考古学財団]]|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher=[[同成社]]|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor=[[神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター]]|title=平成30年（2018年）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=神奈川県教育委員会|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv}}

[[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[明石海峡]]に面した[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]）]]
'''海浜型前方後円墳'''（かいひんがたぜんぽうこうえんふん）とは、[[古墳時代]]の主要な墳形である[[前方後円墳]]の中で、[[リアス式海岸]]の先端や[[海岸段丘]]、[[浜堤|浜堤帯]]など、[[海洋]]の沿岸部に面し、かつ海側からの眺望に優れた場所に意図的に築造されたものを表す呼称である。[[考古学者]]の[[広瀬和雄]]が2013年（平成25年）から2015年（平成27年）ごろ提唱した概念とされる{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==概要== [[前方後円墳]]に代表される古墳時代の古墳について、海に面した場所に築造される事例があることは知られていたが、2013年（平成25年）11月に[[神奈川県]]において[[かながわ考古学財団]]主催で開催された[[考古学]]系シンポジウム「海浜型前方後円墳の時代」に際し、広瀬和雄が「海浜型前方後円墳」の呼称を提起し、その定義について具体的に考証を加えた。このシンポジウム記録は2015年（平成27年）に一般書籍化されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015}}。
広瀬は、海浜型前方後円墳の定義として以下の条件を挙げている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=1-31}}。
#「海浜」に面した場所に造られ、かつ周囲より一段「小高い」位置に立地する。海上からも墳丘が見える位置にあり、かつ交通の要衝に立地する。 #海に突き出た高い土地にあり、[[水稲耕作]]に適した[[沖積平野]]の近くには存在しない（そのような沖積平野を望む位置に造られる前方後円墳とは、生産基盤のあり方が異なるらしい）。 #地域の中でも大型の前方後円墳である（※[[円墳]]など、前方後円墳以外の墳形のものも大型の傾向がある）。 #古墳時代前期（4世紀）の段階で、段築構造・[[埴輪]]列・[[葺石]]など、外表施設・構造物を完備した前方後円墳として出現する。 #それまで古墳が存在しなかった地域に突如現れ、首長系譜が1～2代程度しか継続せず（1基単独か、継続しても2基程度しか築造されない）、系譜的連続性に乏しいものが多い。短い首長系譜が一旦途切れ、空白期間を経て突然復活する例もある。 #地域的遍在性をもつ。
[[西日本]]では[[九州]]東西沿岸部、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[大阪湾]]岸、東日本では[[東京湾]]沿岸、かつて存在した[[香取海]]沿岸、[[東北地方]]の太平洋岸に造られた古墳（群）がこれにあたるとされている。海浜型前方後円墳は、海上からの視認性を重視し、[[入り江]]付近や[[岬]]の突端など、海上交通の要衝と言うべき地を選んで造られていると見られることから、これらを築造した人物は、地域において海運を掌握した[[首長]]層と考えられている。またそれまで古墳を造るような首長のいなかった土地にも突如出現し、短期間で首長系譜が途絶える状況から、築造の背後に[[ヤマト王権|中央の王権]]との強い結び付きがあったとも想定されている{{Sfn|広瀬|2015|pp=21-25}}。
考古学研究者の西川修一は、[[箱式石棺]]墓などの、高塚[[古墳]]以外の墓形・墓制も含めた概念である「海の古墳」よりも範囲を限定した言葉であると位置付けている{{Sfn|西川|2018|p=3}}。
==代表的な例== *[[妙見山古墳 (今治市)|妙見山古墳]]（[[愛媛県]][[今治市]][[大西町]]） *[[五色塚古墳]]（[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[垂水区]]） *[[網野銚子山古墳]]（[[京都府]][[京丹後市]]） *[[長柄桜山古墳群]]（[[神奈川県]][[逗子市]]・[[三浦郡]][[葉山町]]） *[[内裏塚古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[富津市]]） *[[姉崎古墳群]]（[[千葉県]][[市原市]]） *[[磯浜古墳群]]（[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[大洗町]]）
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人[[かながわ考古学財団]]|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher=[[同成社]]|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv}}
*{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor=[[神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター]]|title=平成30年（2018年）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=神奈川県教育委員会|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv}}
==関連項目== *[[前方後円墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:かいひんかたせんほうこうえんふん}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|qq=|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term '''"Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun"''' and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a '''Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun''':{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a '''coastal''' location, typically on '''elevated''' ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are '''not''' located near '''[[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]]''' suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the '''largest kofun''' in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale '''round kofun'''.) # They appear '''fully developed''' in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century)''', featuring '''tiered mound construction (段築構造)''', '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列)''', and [[Fukiishi]] '''(葺石)'''. # These kofun '''suddenly appear''' in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts '''only one or two generations''', meaning that either a '''single mound''' or, at most, '''two kofun''' are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a '''gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges'''. # They exhibit '''regional clustering''', with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In '''Western Japan''', these kofun appear along the '''eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州)''', '''the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海)''', and '''Osaka Bay (大阪湾)'''. In '''Eastern Japan''', they are found along '''Tokyo Bay (東京湾)''', the '''former Katori Sea (香取海)''', and the '''Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方)'''. Given their '''high visibility from the sea''' and strategic locations near '''inlets and capes''', these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled '''maritime trade and navigation'''. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that '''their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権)'''.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is '''more specific''' than broader terms like '''"Marine Kofun" (海の古墳)''', which includes '''other types of tombs''' such as '''box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓)'''.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人{{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|qq=|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|ja|同成社|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei-sha|qq=|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor={{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|qq=|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Archaeological Artifacts Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928683}}|title=平成30th year （2018th year ）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education]]|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]]

These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|qq=|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term '''"Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun"''' and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|qq=|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a '''Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun''':{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a '''coastal''' location, typically on '''elevated''' ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are '''not''' located near '''[[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]]''' suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the '''largest kofun''' in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale '''round kofun'''.) # They appear '''fully developed''' in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century)''', featuring '''tiered mound construction (段築構造)''', '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列)''', and [[Fukiishi]] '''(葺石)'''. # These kofun '''suddenly appear''' in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts '''only one or two generations''', meaning that either a '''single mound''' or, at most, '''two kofun''' are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a '''gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges'''. # They exhibit '''regional clustering''', with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In '''Western Japan''', these kofun appear along the '''eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州)''', '''the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海)''', and '''Osaka Bay (大阪湾)'''. In '''Eastern Japan''', they are found along '''Tokyo Bay (東京湾)''', the '''former Katori Sea (香取海)''', and the '''Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方)'''. Given their '''high visibility from the sea''' and strategic locations near '''inlets and capes''', these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled '''maritime trade and navigation'''. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that '''their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権)'''.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is '''more specific''' than broader terms like '''"Marine Kofun" (海の古墳)''', which includes '''other types of tombs''' such as '''box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓)'''.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term '''"Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun"''' and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a '''Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun''':{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a '''coastal''' location, typically on '''elevated''' ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are '''not''' located near '''[[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]]''' suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the '''largest kofun''' in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale '''round kofun'''.) # They appear '''fully developed''' in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century)''', featuring '''tiered mound construction (段築構造)''', '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列)''', and [[Fukiishi]] '''(葺石)'''. # These kofun '''suddenly appear''' in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts '''only one or two generations''', meaning that either a '''single mound''' or, at most, '''two kofun''' are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a '''gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges'''. # They exhibit '''regional clustering''', with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In '''Western Japan''', these kofun appear along the '''eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州)''', '''the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海)''', and '''Osaka Bay (大阪湾)'''. In '''Eastern Japan''', they are found along '''Tokyo Bay (東京湾)''', the '''former Katori Sea (香取海)''', and the '''Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方)'''. Given their '''high visibility from the sea''' and strategic locations near '''inlets and capes''', these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled '''maritime trade and navigation'''. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that '''their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権)'''.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is '''more specific''' than broader terms like '''"Marine Kofun" (海の古墳)''', which includes '''other types of tombs''' such as '''box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓)'''.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) == <!--Coastal Zenpokoenfun--><!--Coastal Zenpokoenfun--><!--Coastal Zenpokoenfun--> [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Archaeology of Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q116958143}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term '''"Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun"''' and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a '''Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun''':{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a '''coastal''' location, typically on '''elevated''' ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are '''not''' located near '''[[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]]''' suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the '''largest kofun''' in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale '''round kofun'''.) # They appear '''fully developed''' in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century)''', featuring '''tiered mound construction (段築構造)''', '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列)''', and [[Fukiishi]] '''(葺石)'''. # These kofun '''suddenly appear''' in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts '''only one or two generations''', meaning that either a '''single mound''' or, at most, '''two kofun''' are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a '''gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges'''. # They exhibit '''regional clustering''', with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In '''Western Japan''', these kofun appear along the '''eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州)''', '''the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海)''', and '''Osaka Bay (大阪湾)'''. In '''Eastern Japan''', they are found along '''Tokyo Bay (東京湾)''', the '''former Katori Sea (香取海)''', and the '''Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方)'''. Given their '''high visibility from the sea''' and strategic locations near '''inlets and capes''', these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled '''maritime trade and navigation'''. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that '''their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権)'''.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is '''more specific''' than broader terms like '''"Marine Kofun" (海の古墳)''', which includes '''other types of tombs''' such as '''box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓)'''.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人{{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|ja|同成社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei-sha|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor={{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Archaeological Artifacts Center|comment=changed title based revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
[[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]]
'''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}}
== Overview ==
It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term '''"Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun"''' and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}}
{{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a '''Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun''':{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}}
# The mound is constructed at a '''coastal''' location, typically on '''elevated''' ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes.
# These kofun are '''not''' located near '''[[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]]''' suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies.
# They are among the '''largest kofun''' in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale '''round kofun'''.)
# They appear '''fully developed''' in the early '''[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century)''', featuring '''tiered mound construction (段築構造)''', '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列)''', and [[Fukiishi]] '''(葺石)'''.
# These kofun '''suddenly appear''' in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts '''only one or two generations''', meaning that either a '''single mound''' or, at most, '''two kofun''' are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a '''gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges'''.
# They exhibit '''regional clustering''', with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline.
In '''Western Japan''', these kofun appear along the '''eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州)''', '''the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海)''', and '''Osaka Bay (大阪湾)'''. In '''Eastern Japan''', they are found along '''Tokyo Bay (東京湾)''', the '''former Katori Sea (香取海)''', and the '''Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方)'''. Given their '''high visibility from the sea''' and strategic locations near '''inlets and capes''', these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled '''maritime trade and navigation'''. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that '''their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権)'''.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}}
The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is '''more specific''' than broader terms like '''"Marine Kofun" (海の古墳)''', which includes '''other types of tombs''' such as '''box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓)'''.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}}
== Notable Examples ==
* [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]])
* {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]])
* {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]])
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}

*{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor={{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Archaeological Artifacts Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928683}}|title=平成30th year （2018th year ）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education]]|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv|qq=}}

However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}}
{{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun:{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}}
# The mound is constructed at a coastal location, typically on elevated ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes.
# These kofun are not located near [[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]] suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies.
# They are among the largest kofun in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale round kofun.)
# They appear fully developed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century), featuring tiered mound construction (段築構造), [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列), and [[Fukiishi]] (葺石).
# These kofun suddenly appear in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts only one or two generations, meaning that either a single mound or, at most, two kofun are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges.
# They exhibit regional clustering, with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline.
In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}}
The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}}
== Notable Examples ==
* [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]])
* {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]])
* {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]])
* [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]])

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun:{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a coastal location, typically on elevated ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are not located near [[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]] suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the largest kofun in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale round kofun.) # They appear fully developed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century), featuring tiered mound construction (段築構造), [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列), and [[Fukiishi]] (葺石). # These kofun suddenly appear in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts only one or two generations, meaning that either a single mound or, at most, two kofun are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges. # They exhibit regional clustering, with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun:{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a coastal location, typically on elevated ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are not located near [[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]] suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the largest kofun in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale round kofun.) # They appear fully developed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century), featuring tiered mound construction (段築構造), [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列), and [[Fukiishi]] (葺石). # These kofun suddenly appear in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts only one or two generations, meaning that either a single mound or, at most, two kofun are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges. # They exhibit regional clustering, with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人{{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|ja|同成社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei-sha|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor={{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Archaeological Artifacts Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928683}}|title=平成30th year （2018th year ）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education]]|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * [[:en:Archaeology|Archaeology]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun:{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a coastal location, typically on elevated ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are not located near [[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]] suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the largest kofun in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale round kofun.) # They appear fully developed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century), featuring tiered mound construction (段築構造), [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列), and [[Fukiishi]] (葺石). # These kofun suddenly appear in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts only one or two generations, meaning that either a single mound or, at most, two kofun are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges. # They exhibit regional clustering, with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Amino Chōshiyama Kofun|Amino Chōshiyama Kofun]] ([[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster|Nagae-Sakurayama Kofun Cluster]] ([[:en:Zushi, Kanagawa|Zushi, Kanagawa]] / {{ill|Hayama, Kanagawa|ja|三浦郡葉山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama, Kanagawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ([[:en:Futtsu|Futtsu, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|lt=Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|姉崎古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Isohama Kofun Cluster|Isohama Kofun Cluster]] ({{ill|Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja|東茨城郡大洗町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōarai, Ibaraki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]) == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == *{{Cite book|和書|last=広瀬|first=和雄|authorlink=広瀬和雄|editor=公益財団法人{{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}|title=海浜型前方後円墳の時代|publisher={{ill|Dōsei-sha|ja|同成社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōsei-sha|1=Dōsei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11414650}}|year=2015|date=2015-03-31|isbn=9784886216922|ref=harv|qq=}} *{{Cite book|和書|last=西川|first=修一|editor={{ill|Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|ja|神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Prefectural Buried Cultural Properties Center|1=Kanagawa Prefectural Archaeological Artifacts Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30928683}}|title=平成30th year （2018th year ）度第6回考古学講座・古墳時代の海洋民について|series=神奈川県埋蔵文化財センター考古学講座配布資料|publisher=[[:en:Kanagawa Prefectural Board of Education|Kanagawa Prefecture Board of Education]]|year=2018|date=2018-12-22|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/docs/ar3/cnt/f6656/p893306.html|ref=harv|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海浜型前方後円墳]] {{Infobox ancient site}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q116958143]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Goshiki-zuka Kofun, zenpoubu-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|[[:en:Akashi Strait|Akashi Strait]] overlooking [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]] ({{ill|Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja|神戸市垂水区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi-ku, Kobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]])]] '''{{ill|Coastal Zenpokoenfun|ja|海浜型前方後円墳|1=Coastal Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Coastal Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116958143}}''' (海浜型前方後円墳) refers to a subset of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], the predominant Kofun type of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], that were deliberately constructed at the tips of [[:en:Ria|Ria coast]] formations, {{ill|Marine terrace|ja|海岸段丘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marine terrace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=marine terrace|WD=Q3518589}} edges, or [[:en:Beach ridge|Beach ridge]] zones. These tombs were positioned along coastlines with excellent visibility from the sea. The concept was proposed by the archaeologist {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} between 2013 and 2015.{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Overview == It was previously recognized that some [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] were built facing the sea. However, during a 2013 symposium titled *The Age of Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun*, organized by the {{ill|Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|ja|かながわ考古学財団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Archaeology Foundation|1=Kanagawa Archeology Foundation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30930414}}, {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} introduced the term "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" and further defined its characteristics. The symposium’s findings were later published as a general reference book in 2015.{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|qq=}} {{ill|Hirose Kazuo|ja|広瀬和雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirose Kazuo|1=Kazuo Hirose|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485567}} outlined the following defining conditions for a Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun:{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=1-31|qq=}} # The mound is constructed at a coastal location, typically on elevated ground, making it visible from the sea and situated at a key point along maritime transportation routes. # These kofun are not located near [[:en:Alluvial_plains|alluvial plains]] suitable for {{ill|Rice Cultivation|ja|稲作|lt=wet rice cultivation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rice Cultivation|qq=}}, distinguishing them from inland Kofuns that were closely tied to agricultural economies. # They are among the largest kofun in their respective regions. (This trend also applies to other mound shapes, such as large-scale round kofun.) # They appear fully developed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century), featuring tiered mound construction (段築構造), [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] rows (埴輪列), and [[Fukiishi]] (葺石). # These kofun suddenly appear in regions with no previous history of large Kofuns. The ruling lineage associated with them typically lasts only one or two generations, meaning that either a single mound or, at most, two kofun are built before the lineage disappears. In some cases, there is a gap in time before a new ruling lineage emerges. # They exhibit regional clustering, with prominent examples found along Japan’s coastline. In Western Japan, these kofun appear along the eastern and western coasts of Kyushu (九州), the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海), and Osaka Bay (大阪湾). In Eastern Japan, they are found along Tokyo Bay (東京湾), the former Katori Sea (香取海), and the Pacific coastline of the Tōhoku region (東北地方). Given their high visibility from the sea and strategic locations near inlets and capes, these tombs are thought to have belonged to rulers who controlled maritime trade and navigation. Additionally, because they often appear in regions without a previous tradition of mound-building and vanish quickly, it is speculated that their construction was heavily influenced by the central authority of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato state]] (ヤマト王権).{{Sfn|Hirose|2015|pp=21-25|qq=}} The archaeologist {{ill|Nishikawa Shūichi|ja|西川修一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikawa Shūichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that the concept of "Coastal Keyhole-shaped Kofun" is more specific than broader terms like "Marine Kofun" (海の古墳), which includes other types of tombs such as box-shaped stone cist tombs (箱式石棺墓).{{Sfn|Nishikawa|2018|p=3|qq=}} == Notable Examples == * [[:en:Myōkensan Kofun|Myōkensan Kofun]] ({{ill|Ōnishi, Imabari|ja|今治市大西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnishi, Imabari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ehime Prefecture|Ehime Prefecture]]) * [[:en:Goshikizuka (Sentsubo) Kofun|Goshikizuka Kofun]]
